title: Desktop mobile glasses and ambient client families defined
objective: Define the four LifeOS presentation clients — desktop-first, then later-approved mobile, glasses, and ambient — such that they present one coherent LifeOS state, identity, command, notification, and accessibility model, and such that every client carries bounded capabilities, a shared state contract, authentication, privacy, accessibility, offline behavior, notification authority, a test matrix, rollback, and proof. Desktop is decomposed and proven first; mobile, glasses, and ambient remain gated by a proven Rust-native substrate and explicit owner approval. This file is a planning artifact only: no client is implemented, no device is deployed, no account is accessed, and no production release is produced.
design_note: This file IS the design artifact named in target_artifacts (generated/task_families/EXPERIENCE-002.json). Each work_item is a complete per-client presentation contract, not a summary: it carries the full set of gate clauses (bounded_capabilities, shared_state_contract, authentication, privacy, accessibility, offline_behavior, notification_authority, test_matrix, rollback, proof) plus its client status (proven-first vs gated). The five coherence models named in the goal (one state, identity, command, notification, and accessibility model) are factored into cross_cutting_contract so the shared constitution is stated once rather than restated four times, and each client work_item then specializes them.
supporting_sources: generated/task_graph.source.csv (EXPERIENCE-002 row: goal, verification_gate, blocked_paths, execution_cell=policy-cell, simulation_required=TRUE, next_action, rollback_plan)
generated/task_graph.source.csv (EXPERIENCE-001 row: stable Rust-native application/state/command/event/plugin/policy/data APIs that clients consume without owning LifeOS logic)
generated/task_graph.source.csv (LIFEOS-010 row: separate Rust-native substrate from replaceable presentation; every interface operates the same governed LifeOS state)
generated/store_relationship_decision.json (STORE-001: single canonical PostgreSQL + RuVector durable owner; redb transient WAL/scratchpad; forward-only envctl bridge; secrets in OS keyring)

1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv (LifeOS/UDS relation rows; redb read via UDS for real-time agent status; Bun FFI to memory-mapped redb)
generated/task_graph.source.csv (PERSONAL-001 personal identity/privacy/data-ownership family; HOME-001/HOME-002/HOME-003 home domain contracts the ambient client presents)

DESIGN.md and CLAUDE.md (LifeOS Design System: WCAG AA, tests/a11y 32 axe assertions across 9 views + 4 overlays + 6 component variants, Lucide-only iconography, tokens-not-literals, Lexend/JetBrains Mono, dark-first, calm second-person voice)

existing desktop app: src-tauri/src/lib.rs (OS keyring service 'lifeos' with env-var fallback), src/lib/persistence.ts (LIFEOS_PERSIST_KEYS debounced client-local whitelist), src/App.vue shell (Sidebar | Workspace | main | AIAvatar)
client_universe_grounding: The four clients are the exact set named in the EXPERIENCE-002 goal ('desktop-first and later approved mobile, glasses, and ambient clients') and echoed in LIFEOS-010 ('desktop first and later approved mobile, glasses, and ambient surfaces'). No client is invented beyond this set.

cross_cutting_contract.shared_state_model: One coherent LifeOS state. Every client resolves to the single canonical durable owner defined by STORE-001: PostgreSQL + RuVector owns every durable macro-state class exactly once. Clients never read the database directly and never become a second durable writer; they read canonical state ONLY through the EXPERIENCE-001 state/data APIs, and they read transient live status (agent thoughts, swarm status, in-flight traces) from the redb high-frequency tier via Unix Domain Sockets or the shared memory-mapped redb file, exactly as the blueprint specifies ('LifeOS reads redb files via UDS for real-time agent status'; Bun FFI 'reads the same memory-mapped redb files used by the agents'). State is coherent across desktop, mobile, glasses, and ambient precisely because all four resolve to the same PostgreSQL canonical owner — the client is replaceable presentation over a governed substrate (LIFEOS-010), never the system of record.
cross_cutting_contract.identity_model: One identity model. Authentication is per-principal (principal + grant, the governed identity family); credentials live in the OS/platform keyring — the STORE-001 external-secret class stays in the keyring + committed source pins, and PostgreSQL holds only references/provenance, never raw secrets, while redb never holds secrets. The existing desktop app already binds this: the OS keyring service 'lifeos' with an env-var fallback. David is the final human authority (runtime rule); no agent receives unlimited authority; a client authenticates a principal, it does not mint authority.
cross_cutting_contract.command_model: One command model. Every client issues commands through the EXPERIENCE-001 command API into the governed execution spine (identity -> policy -> memory -> simulation -> execution -> proof -> rollback); business logic and authority stay outside the replaceable client (EXPERIENCE-001). Any external-effect command hits the Needs-Human-Approval gate before it can act, and no client may bypass the API to write durable truth directly. Commands mutate durable state only via envctl forward-flush into PostgreSQL (STORE-001 forward-only sync), never by a client writing the canonical store.
cross_cutting_contract.notification_model: One notification authority. Authoritative notifications derive from governed events on the canonical PostgreSQL state, surfaced through the EXPERIENCE-001 event API; clients render and deduplicate these across devices but never invent an authoritative alert. redb-transient status pulses (live swarm/agent status read via UDS) are live-status decoration only and are explicitly not governed notifications — this matches the design contract 'one brand glow per viewport (status pulses do not count)'. Cross-device delivery is deduplicated so two clients do not double-alert (continuity is settled in EXPERIENCE-003).
cross_cutting_contract.accessibility_model: One accessibility model. Every surface conforms to the DESIGN.md accessibility contract: WCAG AA, axe-verified components, Lucide-only iconography, tokens-not-literals, Lexend / JetBrains Mono, dark-first, and the calm, second-person, present-tense, sentence-case voice with 'LifeOS suggests:' for agent recommendations. The desktop client enforces this today (32 axe assertions across 9 views + 4 overlays + 6 component variants, 0 violations, via bun run test:a11y); the gated clients inherit the same model and must pass an equivalent, surface-adapted a11y matrix before approval.
cross_cutting_contract.gating_law: Desktop is decomposed and proven first (EXPERIENCE-002 next_action). Mobile, glasses, and ambient remain gated by two independent conditions: (1) a proven Rust-native substrate — the EXPERIENCE-001 application/state/command/event/plugin/policy/data APIs green and the STORE-001 substrate proven — and (2) explicit owner (David) approval. No gated client is designed into existence, built, or shipped by this task.
cross_cutting_contract.cell_law: EXPERIENCE-002 runs in a policy-cell (execution_cell=policy-cell, simulation_required=TRUE). blocked_paths forbid client implementation, device deployment, account access, production release, and secrets access. This family accesses no device, no account, and no secret; it produces a plan.
cross_cutting_contract.rollback_primitive: Client rollback is safe by construction: because a client owns no durable truth (STORE-001 single durable writer), rolling back or removing a client changes zero durable state. Client-local caches (the desktop LIFEOS_PERSIST_KEYS whitelist, debounced at 300 ms; any mobile/glasses/ambient equivalent) are non-authoritative and regenerable. Canonical rollback is PostgreSQL PITR plus Copy-On-Write branch discard; the task rollback_plan restores the archived client plan.
cross_cutting_contract.proof_primitive: Every client closes through a ProofRecord on the canonical PostgreSQL state. Desktop's proof is decomposed first; the gated clients' proofs are deferred until the substrate is proven and the owner approves, so no gated client is marked done in this task.

work_items.status: active contract — desktop-first, proof decomposed first
work_items.owner: LifeOS Experience Agent under David's final authority
work_items.decision: Adopt the existing LifeOS Tauri 2 + Vue 3 desktop application (App.vue shell: Sidebar | Workspace | main | AIAvatar; six workspaces plus /settings) as the desktop client bound by this presentation contract, and decompose its proof first. It is the fullest-capability client and the reference implementation of the five coherence models; the other three clients are specialized, narrowed variants of it.
work_items.bounded_capabilities: Full presentation and command surface — workspace navigation, dashboard, subsection views, N8n flow view, OpenPencil editing, command palette, and the AI avatar — all issued as governed commands through the EXPERIENCE-001 command API. Bounded out: the client owns no LifeOS logic, no authority, and no durable truth; it renders governed state and requests governed actions.
work_items.shared_state_contract: Reads canonical PostgreSQL + RuVector state through the EXPERIENCE-001 state/data API; reads redb-transient agent/swarm status via UDS and the shared memory-mapped redb file (Bun FFI), per the blueprint. Persists only the non-authoritative LIFEOS_PERSIST_KEYS client-local UI whitelist (activeId, wsCollapsed, sectionByWs, aiProvider, orders, avatar/UI flags); never a durable writer for any PostgreSQL-owned class (STORE-001 invariant).
work_items.authentication: Per-principal via the OS keyring (service 'lifeos') with the documented env-var fallback; PostgreSQL holds references/provenance only, never raw keys; redb never holds secrets (STORE-001 external-secret class). David is the final authority.
work_items.privacy: PERSONAL-001 data-ownership boundary; local-first; Tauri FS scope locked to $APPDATA/lifeos/*; no personal data leaves its boundary without an explicit consent-backed grant; secrets stay in the keyring, never in PostgreSQL or redb.
work_items.accessibility: DESIGN.md WCAG AA is enforced on this client today: 32 axe assertions across 9 dedicated views + 4 overlays + 6 component variants, 0 violations, via bun run test:a11y; Lucide-only iconography; tokens-not-literals; calm second-person voice with 'LifeOS suggests:'.
work_items.offline_behavior: The persistence layer no-ops gracefully outside Tauri and degrades without console errors; client-local UI state is cached (whitelist, debounced 300 ms) and in-flight intent buffers into the redb application-level WAL during PostgreSQL latency/outage; on reconnect envctl forward-flushes only settled state redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL (forward-only, no dual-write); canonical recovery is PostgreSQL PITR, so client-local cache and canonical truth never diverge into a second authority.
work_items.notification_authority: Renders governed events from the EXPERIENCE-001 event API; the AI avatar surfaces 'LifeOS suggests:' governed suggestions; one brand glow per viewport, with redb-transient status pulses treated as live status only, never as authoritative notifications.
work_items.test_matrix: bun run test (Vitest, 65+ specs, happy-dom + @vue/test-utils, dual-mode AI provider path preserved: canned reply under Vitest, invoke('ai_complete') under Tauri); bun run test:a11y (32 axe assertions, 0 violations); bun run build (vue-tsc --noEmit then vite build); dev server mounts #app on :1420 without console errors — the AGENTS.md verification-before-done gate.
work_items.rollback: Restore the prior desktop build/plan; the client-local cache is disposable and regenerable; because the client is non-authoritative, rollback changes no durable state (STORE-001); PostgreSQL PITR remains the canonical restore.
work_items.proof: ProofRecord is decomposed FIRST (EXPERIENCE-002 next_action) and binds the a11y run, the Vitest suite, the build gate, and the shared-state-contract check (state read only through the EXPERIENCE-001 API, redb read only for transient status, no direct durable write).
work_items.source_grounding: EXPERIENCE-002 goal + next_action ('Decompose desktop proof first'); EXPERIENCE-001 API consumption and authority-outside-clients rule; LIFEOS-010 substrate/presentation separation; STORE-001 single durable owner + keyring secret class + redb WAL; blueprint UDS/mmap redb status read; DESIGN.md/CLAUDE.md a11y and voice; existing App.vue, persistence.ts (LIFEOS_PERSIST_KEYS), lib.rs (keyring 'lifeos').

work_items.status: gated — deferred pending proven substrate + explicit owner approval
work_items.owner: LifeOS Experience Agent under David's final authority
work_items.decision: Define the later-approved, touch-first mobile client as a deliberately narrowed variant of the desktop reference: it views governed state, approves or denies human-approval-gated actions, and captures quick input, but does not carry the full editing surface at first. It stays gated until the substrate is proven and the owner approves.
work_items.bounded_capabilities: View governed state, approve/deny Needs-Human-Approval actions, and quick capture; no full OpenPencil editing surface at first; command-only through the EXPERIENCE-001 command API; owns no LifeOS logic, authority, or durable truth.
work_items.shared_state_contract: Reads the same canonical PostgreSQL + RuVector state through the EXPERIENCE-001 state/data API and receives live status through the governed event stream (UDS where the substrate runs on-device, otherwise the governed remote state API); non-authoritative; never a second durable writer. Coherent with desktop because it resolves to the identical canonical owner.
work_items.authentication: Per-principal, mapped to the same governed identity as desktop, with credentials in the mobile platform keystore (never in PostgreSQL or redb); step-up authentication required to approve external-effect actions; David remains final authority.
work_items.privacy: PERSONAL-001 boundary plus the mobile device-loss risk: local-first and encrypted, and safe to lose or remote-wipe precisely because the device holds no durable truth (non-authoritative); no silent client-to-client exfiltration — cross-device movement is a consent-backed grant through the governed substrate.
work_items.accessibility: Same DESIGN.md model, mobile-adapted (touch-target sizing, screen-reader parity, dynamic type); must pass an equivalent axe/a11y matrix before approval.
work_items.offline_behavior: Offline is common on mobile: read the last-known governed state cache; buffer captured intents into a local redb WAL; on reconnect forward-flush settled state redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL; PostgreSQL PITR is the canonical recovery; cross-device conflict is reconciled by EXPERIENCE-003 continuity, never by the client claiming authority.
work_items.notification_authority: A candidate primary push surface, but notifications still derive from governed PostgreSQL events; deliveries are deduplicated against desktop and other clients so no double-alert occurs; the client never invents an authoritative alert.
work_items.test_matrix: Defined but gated — unit, a11y (mobile-adapted axe), offline WAL buffer + forward-only reconcile recovery, command-authority (no direct durable write), push deduplication, and cross-device continuity; runs only once the client is owner-approved and built.
work_items.rollback: Uninstalling or rolling back the mobile client changes no durable state (non-authoritative); restore the archived mobile plan; PostgreSQL PITR is the canonical restore.
work_items.proof: Deferred — the ProofRecord is gated behind a proven substrate and explicit owner approval (EXPERIENCE-002 next_action); not marked done in this task.
work_items.source_grounding: EXPERIENCE-002 goal ('later approved ... mobile'); EXPERIENCE-001 API consumption; LIFEOS-010 replaceable presentation; STORE-001 single durable owner + keyring + redb WAL; PERSONAL-001 privacy; DESIGN.md a11y; EXPERIENCE-003 continuity for cross-device conflict.

work_items.status: gated — deferred pending proven substrate + explicit owner approval
work_items.owner: LifeOS Experience Agent under David's final authority
work_items.decision: Define the later-approved heads-up glasses client as the most tightly bounded variant: glanceable rendering of a single governed state card plus explicit voice/gesture confirm of a pre-simulated, human-approval-gated step. Because glasses carry always-on camera and microphone, this client has the strictest capture/consent boundary of the four. It stays gated until the substrate is proven and the owner approves.
work_items.bounded_capabilities: Glance-view one governed state card and explicitly confirm a single pre-simulated Needs-Human-Approval step by voice or gesture; no editing, no bulk actions, no free command entry; owns no LifeOS logic, authority, or durable truth.
work_items.shared_state_contract: Reads canonical PostgreSQL + RuVector state through the EXPERIENCE-001 state/data API by way of a paired, already-authenticated host (or the governed remote API), and receives live status through the governed event stream; non-authoritative; never a durable writer.
work_items.authentication: Bound to a paired principal device that holds the credentials; the glasses hold no durable secret and authenticate per-session with an on-face/presence check; David remains final authority; every confirm still routes through the governed command API.
work_items.privacy: Highest sensitivity of the four: always-on camera and microphone are an ambient-capture risk, so capture/recording is off by default and requires an explicit consent-backed grant (PERSONAL-001); no bystander data is retained; secrets never reside on the device; the client renders governed state without recording the environment.
work_items.accessibility: Same DESIGN.md model adapted for heads-up use — audio-first with captions, high-contrast tokens, and a required non-voice fallback path — verified by an a11y matrix before approval.
work_items.offline_behavior: Minimal on-device state; when the substrate is unreachable the client degrades to an explicit 'no live state' rather than presenting stale-as-authoritative data; only ephemeral acknowledgements buffer into the paired host's redb WAL and forward-flush through the host on reconnect; PostgreSQL PITR is the canonical recovery.
work_items.notification_authority: Extremely conservative glanceable rendering of governed PostgreSQL events only, deduplicated with the paired host; a heads-up alert is always derived from a governed event, never invented by the client.
work_items.test_matrix: Defined but gated — glance render, voice/gesture confirm authority (no direct durable write), capture-off-by-default and consent enforcement, a11y with voice + non-voice fallback, and host deduplication; runs only once owner-approved and built.
work_items.rollback: Removing or rolling back the glasses client changes no durable state (non-authoritative); restore the archived glasses plan; PostgreSQL PITR is the canonical restore.
work_items.proof: Deferred — the ProofRecord is gated behind a proven substrate and explicit owner approval; not marked done in this task.
work_items.source_grounding: EXPERIENCE-002 goal ('later approved ... glasses'); EXPERIENCE-001 API consumption and authority-outside-clients; STORE-001 single durable owner + keyring secret class + redb WAL; PERSONAL-001 privacy/consent (ambient-sensor sensitivity); DESIGN.md a11y model.

work_items.status: gated — deferred pending proven substrate + explicit owner approval
work_items.owner: LifeOS Experience Agent under David's final authority
work_items.decision: Define the later-approved ambient client for shared home surfaces (shared displays, voice, home-node) as a presence-based, shared-surface variant that reads the HOME-001 verified home twin and defaults to non-personal information, requires per-viewer identity to surface personal data, and takes no autonomous physical action. It stays gated until the substrate is proven and the owner approves.
work_items.bounded_capabilities: Presence/ambient rendering of shared, non-personal state plus voice commands routed through the EXPERIENCE-001 command API; no per-person private data on a shared surface without per-viewer identity; no autonomous physical action — physical control inherits the HOME-003 explicit-approval + emergency-stop ladder; owns no LifeOS logic, authority, or durable truth.
work_items.shared_state_contract: Reads canonical PostgreSQL + RuVector state through the EXPERIENCE-001 state/data API and reads the HOME-001 verified home digital twin read-only; receives live status through the governed event stream / redb via UDS on the home node; non-authoritative; never a durable writer, and never a physical actuator by itself.
work_items.authentication: Shared-surface identity: renders only non-private ambient state by default and requires per-viewer step-up identity to surface any personal data; David remains final authority; any physical-control command inherits HOME-003 explicit human approval and emergency stop.
work_items.privacy: Highest-exposure shared/observed space: default to a non-personal ambient view, require per-viewer consent to surface personal data (PERSONAL-001), honor the HOME-001 per-asset owner and telemetry-provenance boundary, retain no bystander capture, and keep secrets off the device.
work_items.accessibility: Same DESIGN.md model adapted for ambient/distance use — distance-legible tokens, audio, and no reliance on fine pointer interaction — verified by an a11y matrix before approval.
work_items.offline_behavior: The ambient surface degrades to the last-known non-personal state and buffers nothing personal; only non-personal home-node status buffers into a local redb WAL and forward-flushes on reconnect; PostgreSQL PITR is the canonical recovery; observed physical/twin state is never fabricated when offline.
work_items.notification_authority: May surface governed household notifications that are shared and non-private by default, derived from governed PostgreSQL events, with per-viewer gating for personal notifications and deduplication against personal devices; a notification never triggers autonomous physical actuation.
work_items.test_matrix: Defined but gated — ambient render, per-viewer identity gating of personal data, voice-command authority (no direct durable write, no autonomous physical action), HOME-003 approval + emergency-stop enforcement, and cross-device deduplication; runs only once owner-approved and built.
work_items.rollback: Removing the ambient surface changes no durable state (non-authoritative); restore the archived ambient plan; PostgreSQL PITR is the canonical restore; any physical action (only ever under HOME-003) rolls back via its rehearsed reversal and emergency stop.
work_items.proof: Deferred — the ProofRecord is gated behind a proven substrate, the HOME domain proofs (HOME-001/002/003), and explicit owner approval; not marked done in this task.
work_items.source_grounding: EXPERIENCE-002 goal ('later approved ... ambient'); EXPERIENCE-001 API consumption; LIFEOS-010 substrate/presentation separation; STORE-001 single durable owner + redb WAL via UDS on the home node; HOME-001 verified twin + privacy boundary, HOME-002/HOME-003 no-autonomous-physical-action + emergency-stop; PERSONAL-001 privacy.

execution_order_rationale: Desktop first because it is the proven, existing surface and the reference implementation of the five coherence models (EXPERIENCE-002 next_action decomposes its proof first). The remaining three follow in ascending substrate- and consent-risk order: mobile adds a personal device; glasses add always-on ambient sensors and the strictest capture/consent boundary; ambient adds a shared physical space plus the home twin and physical-control adjacency (HOME-003). Each later client requires strictly more proven substrate and a distinct owner approval, so the order is the gating ladder itself.
store_001_alignment: All four clients resolve to the one canonical PostgreSQL + RuVector durable owner (STORE-001 single durable writer): they read canonical state only through the EXPERIENCE-001 APIs, read redb strictly as the transient high-frequency status/WAL tier via UDS/mmap, and never become a second durable writer. Offline behavior uses redb only as the application-level WAL with forward-only redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL sync and PostgreSQL PITR recovery. Secrets stay in the OS/platform keyring; PostgreSQL holds references/provenance only. Because a client owns no durable truth, client rollback mutates no canonical state. Defining the presentation layer as replaceable, non-authoritative clients over this tiered substrate is building to the intended blueprint spec — an upgrade, not a downgrade.
completion_gate: generated/task_families/EXPERIENCE-002.json defines all four clients (desktop proven-first; mobile, glasses, and ambient gated), and each client carries all ten gate clauses — bounded capabilities, shared state contract, authentication, privacy, accessibility, offline behavior, notification authority, test matrix, rollback, and proof — every clause resolved to the single governed substrate (PostgreSQL canonical owner, redb transient status/WAL, EXPERIENCE-001 APIs); and proof_records/EXPERIENCE-002.proof.json records status pass, product_execution_performed=false, no client built or deployed and no device/account/secret accessed, and STORE-001 alignment.
rollback_plan: Restore the archived client plan; no client or device is changed.
downstream: EXPERIENCE-003 (Cross-interface continuity and accessibility acceptance) consumes these four client contracts and proves the same governed intent, state, approval, action, proof, and recovery remain coherent across the approved interfaces, including conflict handling and cross-device notification deduplication.
EXPERIENCE-001 (Rust-native application/state/command/event/plugin/policy/data APIs) is the stable substrate these clients consume; it must stabilize before any gated client is decomposed further.
HOME-001/HOME-002/HOME-003 gate the ambient client's home-twin read, physical-control approval ladder, and emergency stop.
